---
title: Formula syntax conflict extension test
author: Dongyu Zhao
version: 0.1
date: '2026-05-09'
license: '[CC-BY-SA 4.0](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)'
...

## Formula syntax conflict behavior

These examples define precedence where GitHub formula compatibility and LaTeX
compatibility touch existing Markdown syntax or each other.

Code spans bind before formula delimiters inside the span.

```````````````````````````````` example
Code `$x$` and formula $x$.
.
Document scope=1:1..1:27 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:27 children=5
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="Code " children=0
    ├── Code scope=1:7..1:9 mode=embedded literal="$x$" children=0
    ├── Text scope=1:11..1:23 literal=" and formula " children=0
    ├── Formula scope=1:24..1:26 mode=embedded literal="x" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:27..1:27 literal="." children=0
````````````````````````````````

The GitHub `$`...`$` formula form binds as formula, not as a code span.

```````````````````````````````` example
Formula $`x`$ not code.
.
Document scope=1:1..1:23 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:23 children=3
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:8 literal="Formula " children=0
    ├── Formula scope=1:9..1:13 mode=embedded literal="x" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:14..1:23 literal=" not code." children=0
````````````````````````````````

A same-line display close followed by non-whitespace trailing text is inline
display formula inside a paragraph, not a formula block.

```````````````````````````````` example
$$x$$ trailing
.
Document scope=1:1..1:14 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:14 children=2
    ├── Formula scope=1:1..1:5 mode=standalone literal="x" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:6..1:14 literal=" trailing" children=0
````````````````````````````````

```````````````````````````````` example
\\[x\\] trailing
.
Document scope=1:1..1:16 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:16 children=2
    ├── Formula scope=1:1..1:7 mode=standalone literal="x" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:8..1:16 literal=" trailing" children=0
````````````````````````````````

Indented code takes precedence over formula block openers.

```````````````````````````````` example
    $$x$$
.
Document scope=1:1..1:9 children=1
└── CodeBlock scope=1:5..1:9 mode=standalone info=null language=null literal="$$x$$\n" fenced=false closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````

Non-`formula` fenced code remains ordinary code.

```````````````````````````````` example
```js
$x$
```
.
Document scope=1:1..3:3 children=1
└── CodeBlock scope=1:1..3:3 mode=standalone info="js" language="js" literal="$x$\n" fenced=true closed=true children=0
````````````````````````````````

Code spans also bind before LaTeX backslash delimiters.

```````````````````````````````` example
Code `\\(x\\)` and formula \\(x\\).
.
Document scope=1:1..1:35 children=1
└── Paragraph scope=1:1..1:35 children=5
    ├── Text scope=1:1..1:5 literal="Code " children=0
    ├── Code scope=1:7..1:13 mode=embedded literal="\\\\(x\\\\)" children=0
    ├── Text scope=1:15..1:27 literal=" and formula " children=0
    ├── Formula scope=1:28..1:34 mode=embedded literal="x" children=0
    └── Text scope=1:35..1:35 literal="." children=0
````````````````````````````````
